You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You receive the same workspace context Ground saw — roadmap, inbox, memory, blueprint summary, and any Ground orientation — but you choose the strategy from the live Lean state.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level. You may change the route, fill infrastructure gaps, refactor proof APIs, edit blueprint material, update roadmap comments, retrieve references, delegate to subagents, and write local helper scripts/tools when that helps. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the write scope and shared `references/`, keep changes semantic, and leave no stray scratch files. Prefer ambitious progress over defensive avoidance.

Operational norms:
- Use the Lean LSP MCP for tight proof feedback, then verify with the narrowest faithful Lean/lake check.
- Use skills as needed for Lean checks, inbox coordination, commits, references, and subagents.
- Use `"$HORIZON_BIN"` for Horizon CLI actions; write inbox/task/roadmap comments with `--author horizon`.
- Commit coherent progress yourself with `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>`.
- Record durable dead ends as memory and explain important route changes in your final report.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, archive, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions before proving — the local `horizon search` index plus the Lean LSP MCP search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/state-search/hammer-premise). Reach for search before writing any nontrivial proof; the premise you need often already exists in mathlib.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- references — The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.archon-horizon/runs/0013/sessions/0042-horizon-T9

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

Title: Jacobian⇄Albanese endgame glue

# Opened inbox
- I-0118 issue (by=horizon, to=horizon, labels=agent-ready, projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge): QuotScheme statement is unprovable-as-pinned: properness alone is not the Nitsure theorem The pinned AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme (QuotFunctorDef.lean, final theorem) hypothesizes only [IsProper pi] [LocallyOfFiniteType pi] with arbitrary quasi-coherent L and arbitrary E, but the source theorem ([Nitsure] Sec. 5, thm:quot_representable) requires pi projective, L relatively very ample, E coherent. For merely proper X the Quot/Hilbert functor is in general only an algebraic space (Hironaka's smooth proper non-projective 3-fold is the standard counterexample family), so the pinned statement is false-or-open, not the cited theorem. Same trap genus as the old Grassmannian.representable sk…

# Memory
- **tangentSpaceIso (A.3.iii) is NOT directly provable — critical path runs through the relative Pic functor, and a naive FGA rewire is a FALSE-axiom trap.**

- `Pic0.tangentSpaceIso` (`Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`) cannot be proven against the current foundation: `picSharp` in `FGAPicRepresentability.lean` is `Classical.choice` of a `⟨sorry⟩` `Nonempty` — a fully opaque functor. No H¹ comparison can attach to it. Do not re-probe the sorry directly.
- **Critical path** (in order): (1) ~~real relative Pic presheaf~~ DONE session 0015 (`PicSharp.relPresheaf` in `RelPicFunctor.lean`, `H_T`-coset carrier, axiom-clean); (2) étale sheafification target — Mathlib v4.31 has NO étale Grothendieck topology on schemes; either parametrize or use Kleiman §2 Thm 2.5: for C/k with a **k-rational section**, `Pic^♯(T) → Pic^♯_ét(T)` is already bijective, so representability can be stated against `relPresheaf` directly under a section hypothesis; (3) rewire FGA `picSharp` := (sheafified) relative functor — universe bump `Type u → Type (u+1)` in `HasPicSharp`/`HasPicScheme` is viable (`Functor.RepresentableBy` is universe-polymorphic; `Equiv` is cross-universe); (4) `Pic(C_ε) ≅ H¹(C_ε, O^×)` cocycle classification + truncated exponential split sequence `0 → O_C → O^×_{C_ε} → O^×_C → 1` for the kernel = `H¹(C,O_C)` computation (Kleiman Thm 5.11) — none of this exists yet, largest remaining block.
- **TRAP**: wiring `picSharp := PicSharp.presheaf` (the absolute functor, iso-class carrier) would make `PicSharpRepresentable`'s sorry a mathematically FALSE statement (absolute `Pic(C×T)` is not representable — it differs from `Hom(T, Pic)` by `Pic(T)`). Any rewire must use the relative (and in general sheafified) functor.
- Proof-pattern notes: the `Quotient.lift`/`Quotient.sound` + iso-chain style (pullbackCongr/pullbackId/pullbackComp `.app`, D4′ `pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial`, `pullback_pullback_eq`) compiled essentially first-try — no v4.31 transparency walls in this corner. `pullback.lift_fst/snd` are NOT simp (only `@[reassoc]`); pass them + their `_assoc` forms to `simp` explicitly after `pullback.hom_ext`.
- Milne 3.1/3.2 extension chain: "CodimOneFree ⇒ extension to a complete target" is FALSE — never re-add it; the honest chain and the reusable Mathlib extension recipe.

- **Do not reintroduce** an `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`-style statement (codim-1-free rational map to a complete variety extends): counterexample $\mathbb P^2 \dashrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ away from a point (indeterminacy = one codim-2 point, no regular extension; blow-up needed). Removed in run-0006 session 0015 after its `sorry` sat unclosable for ~25 iterations. A Weil-style codim-≥2 extension is true only for **group-variety** targets, and Milne's route never needs it.
- **Honest chain** (all in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` + `Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`): Milne 3.1 = `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` (proved, valuative); Milne 3.3 (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, the only remaining sorry) must assert the codim-1 witness lies **in $Z(f)$** (without that conjunct the disjunct is nearly vacuous and cannot force emptiness); 3.3 + 3.1 ⇒ $Z(f)=\emptyset$ (`av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`); then extension. **Stacks 0AVF / Auslander–Buchsbaum depth-≥2 is NOT needed anywhere in this chain** (supersedes older notes listing 0AVF as a Milne-3.1 blocker).
- **Recipe: extension from empty indeterminacy** (`existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty`, reduced X / separated Y): witness `X.topIso.inv ≫ X.homOfLE hdom.ge ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom` where `hdom : f.toPartialMap.domain = ⊤` (`toPartialMap.domain = f.domain` is `rfl`); rational-class equality via `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr ⟨⊤, …⟩` + `simp only [PartialMap.restrict_hom, Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap, Scheme.topIso_hom, Scheme.ι_toIso_inv_assoc, Scheme.homOfLE_homOfLE_assoc]`; uniqueness via `hom_ext_of_toRationalMap_eq` (= `PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp` + `Opens.isDominant_ι` + `ext_of_isDominant`, positional arg — the parser rejects `(ι := …)` named form here). Both lemmas need `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` BEFORE the docstring (`(restrict …).domain` / `Hom.toPartialMap` defeq walls).
- **Standing lesson**: before attacking a long-lived typed `sorry`, sanity-check the statement for truth (hunt small counterexamples); a false pinned statement silently blocks a whole cone and can smuggle wrong `\uses` (here it dragged a phantom `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`/0AVF dependency into the blueprint for ~30 iterations).
- **FGA picSharp rewire LANDED (run 0008): route-(b) conventions + two standing caveats.**

State after the rewire (`Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`, **4 real `sorry`s**, all TRUE statements — Ground-verified run 0008 reconcile):

- `picSharp C := PicSharp.relPresheaf C ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat` (`Type (u+1)`-valued, REAL). `Scheme.HasRationalPoint C` = Nonempty section subtype of `C.hom`; the ONE genuine FGA sorry is `instHasPicScheme [HasRationalPoint C]`. The other three are typed-sorry carriers: `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` (Kleiman §4 `th:main`(1), true under the rational point) and the pre-existing Quot-blocked `HasDivFunctor` / `HasAbelMap`.

**Caveat 1 — pointless curves:** `JacobianWitness` (`Jacobian.lean`) requires the abelian variety $J$ to exist UNconditionally, but route (b) only builds `PicScheme` for pointed $C$. For a genus-$\ge 1$ curve without a rational point the eventual `picardJacobianWitness` proof needs Galois/étale descent from a finite extension where $C$ gains a point (or a pointed restatement of the north star). Not blocking anything now — record before anyone tries to close `picardJacobianWitness` directly.

**Caveat 2 — `smoothProperQuotient` statement is WEAKER than Kleiman lm:qt and unprovable as stated:** Mathlib v4.31 has NO quasi-projectivity vocabulary, so hypothesis (2) of lm:qt is missing from the Lean statement; without it the statement is FALSE (Hironaka-type free ℤ/2 action on a smooth proper non-projective 3-fold: smooth proper equivalence relation, étale-quotient sheaf a non-scheme algebraic space). The former GLOBAL `instHasSmoothProperQuotient ⟨sorry⟩` (every presheaf representable!) was deleted this run (Ground-confirmed gone); the class is a use-site hypothesis only. Do NOT reintroduce a global instance, and do NOT try to prove the theorem as stated.
- **`pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (FBC leaf, `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean:186`) is NOT a cheap adjointness win — Ground's recommendation had the variance backwards. Route analysis (session 0013), do not re-derive.**

- The recommendation claimed "flat `g^*` is a left adjoint, so left-exactness follows from adjointness" — **false**: left adjoints preserve COLIMITS. Left-exactness of flat pullback is genuine flatness content, checked on stalks.
- No dodge exists at the presheaf level: `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is a bare `leftAdjoint` with no pointwise description; the sections-level formula `colim_{U ⊇ g(U')} M(U) ⊗_{O(U)} O(U')` is only RIGHT-exact (the ring maps are not flat — flatness lives on stalks). Sheafification + `SheafOfModules.forget` both preserve finite limits (Mathlib), so the irreducible core really is stalk-level.
- **Viable honest route** (est. 400–800 LOC of new infra): (1) combinators exist — `Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_tfae` / `preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/ShortComplex/ExactFunctor.lean`); (2) Mathlib 2026 has module structure on stalks (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Stalk.lean`, `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`) and stalk⊣skyscraper at plain-category level (`Topology/Sheaves/Skyscraper.lean`), plus `mono_iff_stalk_mono`/`isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (`Topology/Sheaves/Stalks.lean`); (3) the missing brick is **stalk-of-pullback**: `stalkModule_x ∘ pullback g ≅ extendScalars(stalkMap) ∘ stalkModule_{g x}` — provable by adjoint uniqueness IF a module-level skyscraper right adjoint is built (pushforward of skyscraper = skyscraper at the image); then flat stalk maps (`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.iff_flat_stalkMap`-adjacent, `RingHom.Flat` appLE definition) + `ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat` (`ModuleCat/Descent.lean`) finish via kernel-comparison-on-stalks.
- **Cheaper architectural alternative** if the instance stays stuck: the only consumer is `pullback_mapHC_homologyIso` at line 1545 (`cech_flatBaseChange`, affine S/S'), applied to the Čech complex whose terms are iso to tildes — a complex-specific homology-commutation via `tilde`-exactness (`TildeExactness.lean`) + `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` could replace the general instance, at the cost of a termwise-tilde chain-iso.
- **A.3 Pic0 cone state after run-0008 T5 — supersedes the run-0005 'IdentityComponent all 9 sorries blocked' memory.**

- **Proved sorry-free**: `Pic0.grpObj` (`Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C))` = `isSubgroupHomomorphism (PicScheme C)`; instances synthesize directly from `groupSchemeStructure` (CommGrpObj parent projection) + the `PicSchemeLocallyOfFiniteType` projection instance; `Pic0Scheme` unfolds by rfl, plain `exact` works). Also `Pic0.geometricallyIrreducible` (specialisation of the substrate), both `isAbelianVariety` assemblies (the `Pic0Scheme`-namespace one MOVED to `Pic0AbelianVariety.lean`), and `tangentSpaceCotangentDual` now hypothesis-free.

1723	  -- * Substep 3: pullback of the local ring at the identity `e ∈ G`:
1724	  --   `Φ` defined at `(x, x)` ⟺ `Φ^* (𝒪_{G,e}) ⊆ 𝒪_{X×X, (x,x)}`. Uses the
1725	  --   `Scheme.RationalMap`-to-function-field machinery (the same gap that
1726	  --   blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` at L744 below).
1727	  -- * Substep 4: on the nonsingular variety `X × X`, the pole divisor
1728	  --   `div(g)_∞` of any non-zero rational function `g ∈ Φ^*(𝒪_{G,e})` is
1729	  --   pure codim-1; its intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1 in
1730	  --   the diagonal (Hartshorne AG 9.2, Krull's principal-ideal theorem).
1731	  --   **Substep 4a (pole-divisor purity) is PROVED (run-0006 T6, session
1732	  --   0019)**: `Albanese/PolePurity.lean` provides
1733	  --   `Scheme.exists_specializes_coheight_eq_one_of_notMem_stalk_range` —
1734	  --   on a locally Noetherian integral scheme with regular stalks (e.g.
1735	  --   `X × X` smooth over `k̄`, via `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`
1736	  --   applied to the product), an `h ∈ K(X×X)` not regular at a point `Q₀`
1737	  --   fails to be regular at some coheight-1 generization `z ⤳ Q₀`. The
1738	  --   proof is Serre-free/UFD-free (elementary swap-pair argument replaces
1739	  --   normality; blueprint node `lem:pole_divisor_purity`, axiom-clean).
1740	  --   **Substep 4b (diagonal intersection)** remains: transport the
1741	  --   coheight-1 pole point of `X × X` through `Δ` to a coheight-1 point
1742	  --   of `X` inside `Z(f)` (Krull height bound for the diagonal, a local
1743	  --   complete intersection of codim `dim X` since `X` is smooth).
1744	  --
1745	  -- Iter-200+ tracked: gated on (a) the function-field-pullback bridge for
1746	  -- `Scheme.RationalMap` (Substeps 1–3: difference map, slice argument,
1747	  -- `Φ^*` of `𝒪_{G,e}`; also blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction`),
1748	  -- (b) Substep 4b, the codim-1 diagonal-intersection bound (Hartshorne
1749	  -- AG 9.2 scheme-level form). Substep 4a is closed by
1750	  -- `Albanese/PolePurity.lean` (imported above).
1751	  sorry
1752	
1753	/-! ## §6. Definedness at a prime-divisor generic point
1754	
1755	A truthful, lightweight reformulation of definedness at a prime-divisor
1756	generic point: `f` is defined at `W.point` iff `f` admits a `PartialMap`
1757	representative whose domain contains `W.point`. This is a definitional
1758	re-shuffle of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`
1759	swapping the order of the two conjuncts under the existential for downstream
1760	ergonomics (so the `toRationalMap = f` conjunct lands first, the way later
1761	witnesses are constructed in this project).
1762	
1763	### Status note (iter-179 Lane D, Path D2)
1764	
1765	The blueprint pin `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` (Hartshorne II.6
1766	pp. 130–131) calls for the genuinely substantive statement
1767	
1768	> `f` is defined at `W.point` iff every regular function on every affine
1769	> open `V ⊆ Y` around `f(W.point)` pulls back to an element of
1770	> `O_{X,W.point}` ⊂ `K(X)` of `ord_W`-value `≥ 0`,
1771	
1772	which requires the pullback machinery
1773	`Scheme.RationalMap → K(Y) → K(X)` and the project's existing
1774	`Scheme.RationalMap.order : X.functionField → ℤ` valuation from
1775	`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`. The Lean-level construction of the
1776	`RationalMap`-to-function-field ring map is **not** shipped by Mathlib
1777	at commit `b80f227`, and building it inside this file would constitute
1778	a substantive new sub-build (cf. `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
1779	on the pullback of a sheaf-of-algebras along a morphism).
1780	
1781	iter-178's `extend_iff_order_nonneg` claimed the substantive statement
1782	in its docstring but only proved this reshuffle; the lemma's name and
1783	documentation are corrected here (auditor 178B's Path D2 honest fallback)
1784	to match the proven content. The `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1]` and
1785	`[IsLocallyNoetherian X.left]` typeclass binders that the substantive
1786	statement would have required are dropped along with the renaming, since
1787	the reshuffle does not use them. The substantive statement is deferred to
1788	a future lemma (working title: `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg`) once
1789	the pullback machinery lands.

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.RationalMapPrecomp
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.RationalMapProd
9	
10	/-!
11	# Milne's difference rational map `Φ = (x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`
12	
13	This file assembles **Sub-step 1** of Milne's Lemma 3.3
14	(`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`, `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`):
15	from a rational map `f : X ⤏ G` into a group variety over an algebraically closed
16	field `k̄`, build the *difference rational map*
17	
18	  `Φ : X ×_{k̄} X ⤏ G`,   `Φ(x, y) = f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`,
19	
20	which is the vehicle for reducing the indeterminacy of `f` to a pole-divisor
21	computation on the nonsingular surface `X ×_{k̄} X`.
22	
23	The construction glues the two rational-map bricks already in the project:
24	
25	* `Scheme.RationalMap.precomp` (`Albanese/RationalMapPrecomp.lean`) — precompose
26	  `f` with the two projections `prᵢ : X ×_{k̄} X ⟶ X`, which are open because
27	  `X.hom` is smooth (hence its base change `prᵢ` is smooth, flat, locally of finite
28	  presentation, universally open, an open map);
29	* `Scheme.RationalMap.prod` (`Albanese/RationalMapProd.lean`) — pair
30	  `f ∘ pr₁` and `f ∘ pr₂` into `X ×_{k̄} X ⤏ G ×_{k̄} G`;
31	
32	and then composes on the right with the **group-object difference morphism**
33	`GrpObj.diff G = (g, h) ↦ g · h⁻¹ : G ⊗ G ⟶ G`, whose underlying scheme morphism
34	`(GrpObj.diff G).left : G ×_{k̄} G ⟶ G` is the honest group law `m ∘ (id × inv)`.
35	
36	The pairing `prod` requires the source `X ×_{k̄} X` to be integral; this is passed
37	as the hypothesis `[IsIntegral (pullback X.hom X.hom)]`. The caller supplies it via
38	the project lemma `isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed` (self-product is smooth over
39	`k̄`, hence reduced) together with `GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace` — the
40	`isIntegral_pullback_self` recipe is spelled out in the module note below and lives
41	in `CodimOneExtension.lean`'s cone (it depends on the standard-smooth chart machinery
42	there, so it is not restated here to keep this file a Mathlib-only leaf).
43	
44	## Main definitions
45	
46	* `CategoryTheory.GrpObj.diff` — the division morphism `G ⊗ G ⟶ G` of a group
47	  object in any cartesian-monoidal category.
48	* `AlgebraicGeometry.grpObjDiffLeft` — its underlying scheme morphism, typed as
49	  `pullback G.hom G.hom ⟶ G.left`.
50	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.differenceRationalMap` — Milne's
51	  `Φ : X ×_{k̄} X ⤏ G`.
52	
53	## Main results
54	
55	* `AlgebraicGeometry.isOpenMap_pullback_fst_self` / `..._snd_self` — the two
56	  projections of the self-product are open maps.
57	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.differenceRationalMap_compHom_over` — the
58	  difference map is a `k̄`-rational map (`Φ.compHom G.hom = (pr₁ ≫ X.hom).toRat`).
59	
60	Sub-steps 2 (`(x, x) ∈ Dom Φ ↔ x ∈ Dom f`) and 4b (diagonal codim-1 Krull bound)
61	remain; they consume `differenceRationalMap` built here.
62	-/
63	
64	set_option autoImplicit false
65	
66	universe u
67	
68	open CategoryTheory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonoidalCategory Limits TopologicalSpace
69	open scoped CategoryTheory.MonObj
70	
71	namespace CategoryTheory.GrpObj
72	
73	variable {C : Type*} [Category C] [CartesianMonoidalCategory C]
74	
75	/-- **Difference / division morphism of a group object.** For a group object `G`
76	in a cartesian-monoidal category, `GrpObj.diff G = fst / snd : G ⊗ G ⟶ G` is the
77	morphism `(g, h) ↦ g · h⁻¹`, i.e. the group law composed with inversion on the
78	second factor. There is no ready-made such morphism in mathlib (`GrpObj.conj` and
79	`GrpObj.commutator` are the only prebuilt `G ⊗ G ⟶ G` maps), so we build it from
80	the hom-set group structure `CategoryTheory.MonObj.Hom.group`. -/
81	noncomputable def diff (G : C) [GrpObj G] : G ⊗ G ⟶ G :=
82	  fst G G / snd G G
83	
84	end CategoryTheory.GrpObj
85	
86	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
87	
88	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
89	
90	/-- The underlying scheme morphism `(GrpObj.diff G).left : G ×_{k̄} G ⟶ G` of the
91	group-object difference morphism, typed directly against `pullback G.hom G.hom`
92	(definitionally `(G ⊗ G).left` via `Over.tensorObj_left`). This coercion avoids the
93	`(G ⊗ G).left` vs `pullback G.hom G.hom` transparency wall when right-composing a
94	rational map with target `pullback G.hom G.hom`. -/
95	noncomputable def grpObjDiffLeft (G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [GrpObj G] :
96	    pullback G.hom G.hom ⟶ G.left :=
97	  (GrpObj.diff G).left
98	
99	/-- The difference morphism sits over `Spec k̄`: `grpObjDiffLeft G ≫ G.hom =
100	pr₁ ≫ G.hom`. This is the reduction that turns `Φ.compHom G.hom` into the structure
101	morphism of `X ×_{k̄} X`; it is `Over.w` of `GrpObj.diff G`. -/
102	lemma grpObjDiffLeft_comp_hom (G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [GrpObj G] :
103	    grpObjDiffLeft G ≫ G.hom = pullback.fst G.hom G.hom ≫ G.hom :=
104	  Over.w (GrpObj.diff G)
105	
106	/-- The first projection `X ×_{k̄} X ⟶ X` is an open map (it is smooth, being a
107	base change of the smooth structure morphism, hence universally open). -/
108	theorem isOpenMap_pullback_fst_self (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [Smooth X.hom] :
109	    IsOpenMap (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom).base :=
110	  (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom).isOpenMap
111	
112	/-- The second projection `X ×_{k̄} X ⟶ X` is an open map. -/
113	theorem isOpenMap_pullback_snd_self (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [Smooth X.hom] :
114	    IsOpenMap (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom).base :=
115	  (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom).isOpenMap
116	
117	namespace Scheme.RationalMap
118	
119	variable {X G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
120	  [Smooth X.hom]
121	  [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
122	
123	/-- **Milne's difference rational map** `Φ = (x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`.
124	
125	For a rational map `f : X ⤏ G` into a group variety over `k̄`, with `f` defined
126	over `k̄` (`f.compHom G.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap`), the difference map
127	`Φ : X ×_{k̄} X ⤏ G` is `((f ∘ pr₁) ×_{k̄} (f ∘ pr₂)) ∘ (g, h ↦ g · h⁻¹)`.
128	
129	The self-product must be integral for the pairing `prod` to be well-defined; this
130	is the hypothesis `[IsIntegral (pullback X.hom X.hom)]`. -/
131	noncomputable def differenceRationalMap
132	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left)
133	    (hover : f.compHom G.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap)
134	    [IsIntegral (pullback X.hom X.hom)] :
135	    (pullback X.hom X.hom).RationalMap G.left :=
136	  (RationalMap.prod (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom ≫ X.hom) G.hom G.hom
137	      (f.precomp (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom) (isOpenMap_pullback_fst_self X))
138	      (f.precomp (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom) (isOpenMap_pullback_snd_self X))
139	      (by rw [RationalMap.precomp_compHom, hover, RationalMap.precomp_hom_toRationalMap])
140	      (by rw [RationalMap.precomp_compHom, hover, RationalMap.precomp_hom_toRationalMap,
141	        pullback.condition])).compHom (grpObjDiffLeft G)
142	
143	/-- **The difference map is a `k̄`-rational map.** Right-composing `Φ` with the
144	structure morphism `G.hom` recovers the structure morphism `pr₁ ≫ X.hom` of
145	`X ×_{k̄} X`. -/
146	theorem differenceRationalMap_compHom_over
147	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left)
148	    (hover : f.compHom G.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap)
149	    [IsIntegral (pullback X.hom X.hom)] :
150	    (differenceRationalMap f hover).compHom G.hom
151	      = (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom ≫ X.hom).toRationalMap := by
152	  simp only [differenceRationalMap]
153	  rw [RationalMap.compHom_compHom, grpObjDiffLeft_comp_hom]
154	  exact RationalMap.prod_compHom_over _ _ _ _ _ _ _
155	
156	end Scheme.RationalMap
157	
158	end AlgebraicGeometry
159

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Function-field pullback of a rational map
10	
11	For a rational map `f : X ⤏ Y` out of an integral scheme `X`, Mathlib's
12	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.fromFunctionField` gives a morphism
13	`Spec K(X) ⟶ Y` (the restriction of `f` to the generic point). Composing it with
14	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo` produces the pullback of germs
15	`𝒪_{Y, f(η_X)} ⟶ K(X)` at the image of the generic point of `X`.
16	
17	When `f` is **dominant** and `Y` is irreducible, that image point is the generic
18	point of `Y`, so the germ pullback becomes a field homomorphism
19	`K(Y) ⟶ K(X)` — the function-field functoriality `K(Y) → K(X)` that Milne's
20	rational-map extension leg (`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`) and the
21	Weil-divisor obstruction (`thm:weil_divisor_obstruction`) both require.
22	
23	## Main definitions
24	
25	* `Scheme.RationalMap.stalkPullback` — the germ pullback
26	  `𝒪_{Y, f(η_X)} ⟶ K(X)` (no dominance needed).
27	* `Scheme.RationalMap.fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint` — for a dominant
28	  rational map into an irreducible target, `f(η_X) = η_Y`.
29	* `Scheme.RationalMap.functionFieldPullback` — the induced field homomorphism
30	  `K(Y) ⟶ K(X)` for a dominant rational map.
31	-/
32	
33	set_option autoImplicit false
34	
35	universe u
36	
37	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace IsLocalRing
38	
39	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
40	
41	namespace Scheme
42	
43	namespace RationalMap
44	
45	variable {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
46	
47	/-- **Germ pullback along a rational map.** For a rational map `f : X ⤏ Y` out of
48	an integral scheme `X`, the associated morphism `f.fromFunctionField : Spec K(X) ⟶ Y`
49	induces, by `Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo`, a local ring homomorphism from the germ
50	of `Y` at the image `f(η_X)` of the generic point of `X` into the function field
51	`K(X)`. -/
52	noncomputable def stalkPullback [IsIntegral X] (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
53	    Y.presheaf.stalk (f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint X.functionField)) ⟶ X.functionField :=
54	  Scheme.stalkClosedPointTo f.fromFunctionField
55	
56	/-!
57	## Dominant refinement
58	
59	When `f` is **dominant** and `Y` is irreducible, the base point
60	`f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint K(X))` equals `genericPoint Y`, so `stalkPullback`
61	becomes a field homomorphism `K(Y) ⟶ K(X)`.
62	-/
63	
64	/-- **For a dominant rational map into an irreducible target, the function-field
65	morphism sends the closed point of `Spec K(X)` to the generic point of `Y`.**
66	
67	Reducing to a representative partial map `g`, the morphism
68	`g.fromFunctionField = g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (η_X) ≫ g.hom` sends the closed
69	point of `Spec 𝒪_{X, η_X} = Spec K(X)` to `g.hom (η_{g.domain})`. The inner point
70	is the generic point of the domain (checked after applying the open immersion
71	`g.domain.ι`, using `Opens.fromSpecStalkOfMem_ι` and `fromSpecStalk_closedPoint`),
72	and a dominant morphism sends the generic point of its (irreducible) source to the
73	generic point of `Y` (image of a generic point is generic on the dense range, and
74	generic points are unique in the T₀ space `Y`). -/
75	theorem fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint [IsIntegral X] [IrreducibleSpace Y]
76	    (f : X.RationalMap Y) [f.IsDominant] :
77	    f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint X.functionField) = genericPoint Y := by
78	  obtain ⟨g, rfl⟩ := f.exists_rep
79	  haveI : IsDominant g.hom := (PartialMap.isDominant_toRationalMap_iff g).mp ‹_›
80	  have hη : genericPoint X ∈ g.domain :=
81	    (genericPoint_specializes _).mem_open g.domain.2 g.dense_domain.nonempty.choose_spec
82	  haveI : Nonempty (g.domain : Type _) := ⟨⟨genericPoint X, hη⟩⟩
83	  haveI : IsIntegral g.domain.toScheme := inferInstance
84	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace g.domain.toScheme := inferInstance
85	  -- reduce the rational-map `fromFunctionField` to the partial-map one, then to the
86	  -- composite `g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (η_X) hη ≫ g.hom`
87	  rw [RationalMap.fromFunctionField_toRationalMap]
88	  have hcomp : PartialMap.fromFunctionField g
89	      = g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (genericPoint X) hη ≫ g.hom := rfl
90	  rw [hcomp, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
91	  -- the inner point is the generic point of the domain
92	  have hp : (g.domain.fromSpecStalkOfMem (genericPoint X) hη) (closedPoint X.functionField)
93	      = genericPoint g.domain.toScheme := by
94	    apply (Scheme.Opens.ι g.domain).isOpenEmbedding.injective
95	    rw [genericPoint_eq_of_isOpenImmersion (Scheme.Opens.ι g.domain),
96	      ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, Opens.fromSpecStalkOfMem_ι]
97	    exact Scheme.fromSpecStalk_closedPoint
98	  rw [hp]
99	  -- a dominant morphism sends the generic point of an irreducible source to `genericPoint Y`
100	  have himg := (genericPoint_spec g.domain.toScheme).image g.hom.continuous
101	  rw [Set.image_univ, g.hom.denseRange.closure_range] at himg
102	  exact himg.eq (genericPoint_spec Y)
103	
104	/-- **The induced field homomorphism `K(Y) ⟶ K(X)` of a dominant rational map.**
105	
106	Since `f.fromFunctionField (closedPoint K(X)) = genericPoint Y`
107	(`fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint`), the germ pullback `stalkPullback` has
108	source `𝒪_{Y, η_Y} = K(Y)` up to the canonical stalk isomorphism, giving the
109	function-field functoriality `K(Y) → K(X)` required by the rational-map extension
110	leg of Milne's Albanese argument. -/
111	noncomputable def functionFieldPullback [IsIntegral X] [IrreducibleSpace Y]
112	    (f : X.RationalMap Y) [f.IsDominant] :
113	    Y.functionField ⟶ X.functionField :=
114	  (Y.presheaf.stalkCongr
115	    (Inseparable.of_eq (fromFunctionField_base_eq_genericPoint f).symm)).hom ≫ f.stalkPullback
116	
117	end RationalMap
118	
119	end Scheme
120	
121	end AlgebraicGeometry
122

- §1 Overview and definitions — p.85
- §2 The canonical maps from C to its Jacobian variety — p.91
- §3 The symmetric powers of a curve — p.94

      TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.left.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
    haveI : IsLocalization.AtPrime (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)
        (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzV⟩).asIdeal := hV.isLocalization_stalk ⟨z, hzV⟩
    exact isReduced_localizationPreserves
      (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzV⟩).asIdeal.primeCompl (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) hSred
  exact isReduced_of_isReduced_stalk X.left

/-- **Integrality of the self-product `X ×_{k̄} X`.** For a smooth, geometrically
irreducible, integral scheme `X` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, the self
fibre product `X ×_{k̄} X` is integral: it is reduced because it is smooth over the
algebraically closed field (`isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed` on the composite
structure map `pr₁ ≫ X.hom`) and irreducible because `X.hom` is geometrically
irreducible and universally open (`GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace`,
base change of geometric irreducibility along the open projection).

This discharges the `[IsIntegral (pullback X.hom X.hom)]` hypothesis of
`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.differenceRationalMap` (Milne Lemma 3.3,
Sub-step 1, `Albanese/DifferenceMap.lean`), and supplies the integral-surface input
to the pole-purity engine of Sub-step 4 (`Albanese/PolePurity.lean`). Axiom-clean. -/
theorem isIntegral_pullback_self
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom] [IsIntegral X.left] :
    IsIntegral (pullback X.hom X.hom) := by
  -- The composite structure map `X ×_{k̄} X → Spec k̄` is smooth (`smooth_comp` of
  -- the base-change-smooth projection with `X.hom`); package it as an `Over` object.
  haveI hsm : Smooth (Over.mk (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom ≫ X.hom)).hom := by
    show Smooth (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom ≫ X.hom); infer_instance
  haveI hred : IsReduced (pullback X.hom X.hom) :=
    isReduced_of_smooth_of_isAlgClosed (Over.mk (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom ≫ X.hom))
  -- Irreducibility of `X ×_{k̄} X` from geometric irreducibility of `X.hom` (the
  -- projection is universally open). Drive synthesis through the `Scheme`-typed
  -- application so the `Scheme → Type` coercion on the pullback is inserted (a bare
  -- `IrreducibleSpace (pullback …)` mis-resolves `pullback` in the `Type` category).
  haveI : UniversallyOpen X.hom := inferInstance
  exact isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced (pullback X.hom X.hom)

/-! ## §3.C. Project-local Mathlib supplement — Matsumura regular-sequence bridge (iter-203, Step A1)

Lane COE Step A1 substrate (Matsumura *Commutative Ring Theory* Thm 14.2 /
Stacks 00NQ). The goal of this section is the criterion: on a regular local
Noetherian ring `(A, 𝔪)`, a finite sequence `f₁,…,f_c ∈ 𝔪` whose images in the
cotangent space `𝔪/𝔪²` are `κ`-linearly independent forms a
`RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular` sequence.

The induction is on the length `c`, peeling the head `f₁`. The mathematical
peeling step uses `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.cons'`, whose tail lives over
the *module* quotient `QuotSMulTop f₁ A = A ⧸ (f₁ • ⊤)`. The two bridges below
convert that module-quotient bookkeeping into the *ring* quotient
`A ⧸ Ideal.span {f₁}` (over which the induction hypothesis is naturally
phrased): `quotSMulTop_quotientRing_linearEquiv` is the canonical
`(A ⧸ span{f₁})`-linear identification `QuotSMulTop f₁ A ≃ₗ A ⧸ span{f₁}`, and
`isRegular_cons_of_quotient_ring` is the resulting clean cons rule.

These are project-local because Mathlib ships only the `QuotSMulTop`-flavoured
`IsRegular.cons'`; the ring-quotient repackaging is what makes a downstream
ring-theoretic induction (the Matsumura criterion) ergonomic. -/

/-- **Step A1 bridge (iter-203).** The canonical `(A ⧸ span{r})`-linear
equivalence between the module quotient `QuotSMulTop r A = A ⧸ (r • ⊤)` and the
ring quotient `A ⧸ span{r}`. Built by composing Mathlib's
`QuotSMulTop.equivQuotTensor` (`QuotSMulTop r A ≃ₗ[A] (A⧸span{r}) ⊗[A] A`) with
`TensorProduct.rid` and then promoting the resulting `A`-linear equivalence to
an `(A⧸span{r})`-linear one via `LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfSurjective` along the
surjective quotient map. Axiom-clean. -/
private noncomputable def quotSMulTop_quotientRing_linearEquiv

273	    intro x hx

275	    have h2 := indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete f hover z hzZ
276	    rw [hz1] at h2
277	    norm_num at h2

279	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.2 — a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an
280	abelian variety extends, uniquely, to a regular morphism.**
281	
282	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, and
283	`A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` has a
284	*unique* regular extension `g : X ⟶ A` (i.e. `g.toRationalMap = f`).

293

295	   (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` — the single remaining
296	   `sorry` of this chain) with the codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1
297	   (`indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete`, proved axiom-clean) to
298	   force `indeterminacyLocus f = ∅`.

300	2. `existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty` (proved axiom-clean in
301	   `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): a rational map from a reduced scheme
302	   to a separated scheme with empty indeterminacy locus is uniquely
303	   represented by a regular morphism. The scheme-level separatedness
304	   `A.left.IsSeparated` is derived from `IsSeparated A.hom` (from
305	   `IsProper A.hom`) composed with the affine `Spec k̄ ⟶ ⊤_ Scheme`.
306	
307	The proof body therefore introduces NO new inline `sorry`: all missing
308	content is concentrated in Milne Lemma 3.3. -/
309	theorem extend_to_av
310	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
311	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
312	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
313	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
314	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
315	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
316	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
317	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
318	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left)
319	    (hover : f.compHom A.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap) :
320	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ A.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by
321	  -- Scheme-level separatedness of the target (`A.hom` is separated since
322	  -- proper, and `Spec k̄` is affine hence separated over the terminal).
323	  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
324	  haveI : A.left.IsSeparated := by
325	    rw [Scheme.isSeparated_iff]
326	    have heq : terminal.from A.left = A.hom ≫ terminal.from _ :=
327	      Subsingleton.elim _ _
328	    rw [heq]; infer_instance
329	  -- Milne's argument: the indeterminacy locus is empty, then extend.
330	  exact existsUnique_hom_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty f
331	    (av_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty f hover)
332	
333	end RationalMap

1	/-

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Precomposition of a rational map with an open morphism
10	
11	Mathlib's `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap` API provides only `compHom`,
12	the composition of a rational map with a *morphism on the right*
13	(`f : X ⤏ Y`, `g : Y ⟶ Z` ↦ `X ⤏ Z`). The dual operation — precomposing a
14	rational map with a morphism *on the left* — is missing.
15	
16	For a morphism `p : W ⟶ X` whose underlying continuous map is **open**, and a
17	rational map `f : X ⤏ Y`, the composite `f ∘ p : W ⤏ Y` is a well-defined
18	rational map. Openness of `p` is exactly what guarantees that the preimage
19	`p⁻¹(f.domain)` of the (dense open) domain of definition stays dense
20	(`Dense.preimage`), so that the pulled-back partial map is again a partial map,
21	and that the construction descends to equivalence classes.
22	
23	This is the first missing brick in Milne's difference-map construction
24	`Φ = m ∘ (f × f) : X × X ⤏ G` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Lemma 3.3): the two
25	projections `prᵢ : X × X ⟶ X` are open (flat, locally of finite presentation,
26	being base changes of the smooth structure morphism `X ⟶ Spec k̄`), so
27	`f.precomp prᵢ` is the pullback of `f` along a projection.
28	
29	## Main definitions
30	
31	* `Scheme.PartialMap.precomp` — precomposition of a partial map with an open
32	  morphism.
33	* `Scheme.RationalMap.precomp` — precomposition of a rational map with an open
34	  morphism, descended from `PartialMap.precomp`.
35	
36	## Main results
37	
38	* `Scheme.RationalMap.precomp_compHom` — precomposition commutes with
39	  right-composition, `(f.precomp p hp).compHom g = (f.compHom g).precomp p hp`.
40	-/

56	/-- **Auxiliary restriction square.** For a morphism `p : W ⟶ X` and opens
57	`V ≤ U` of `X`, restricting `p ∣_ U` to the smaller preimage `p ⁻¹ᵁ V` agrees
58	with `p ∣_ V` followed by the inclusion `V ↪ U`. Both sides are pinned down by
59	composing with the mono `U.ι`. -/
60	lemma homOfLE_comp_morphismRestrict (p : W ⟶ X) {U V : X.Opens} (hUV : V ≤ U)
61	    (h : p ⁻¹ᵁ V ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
62	    W.homOfLE h ≫ (p ∣_ U) = (p ∣_ V) ≫ X.homOfLE hUV := by
63	  rw [← cancel_mono U.ι, Category.assoc, morphismRestrict_ι,
64	    ← Category.assoc (W.homOfLE h), Scheme.homOfLE_ι, Category.assoc,
65	    Scheme.homOfLE_ι, morphismRestrict_ι]
66	
67	/-- **Precomposition of a partial map with an open morphism.** For `f : X ⤏ Y`
68	(as a partial map) and a morphism `p : W ⟶ X` whose underlying map is open, the
69	composite `f ∘ p : W ⤏ Y` is defined on `p⁻¹(f.domain)`, which is dense because
70	open maps pull dense sets back to dense sets. -/
71	@[simps domain hom]
72	noncomputable def precomp (f : X.PartialMap Y) (p : W ⟶ X) (hp : IsOpenMap p.base) :
73	    W.PartialMap Y where
74	  domain := p ⁻¹ᵁ f.domain
75	  dense_domain := f.dense_domain.preimage hp
76	  hom := (p ∣_ f.domain) ≫ f.hom
77	
78	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
79	/-- Precomposition respects the equivalence of partial maps, hence descends to
80	rational maps. -/
81	lemma precomp_equiv {f g : X.PartialMap Y} (p : W ⟶ X) (hp : IsOpenMap p.base)
82	    (h : f.equiv g) : (f.precomp p hp).equiv (g.precomp p hp) := by
83	  obtain ⟨V, hV, hVl, hVr, e⟩ := h
84	  have hVd : Dense ((p ⁻¹ᵁ V : W.Opens) : Set W) := by
85	    rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.map_coe]; exact hV.preimage hp
86	  refine ⟨p ⁻¹ᵁ V, hVd, p.preimage_mono hVl, p.preimage_mono hVr, ?_⟩
87	  simp only [restrict_hom, precomp_hom] at e ⊢
88	  rw [← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc,
89	    homOfLE_comp_morphismRestrict p hVl, homOfLE_comp_morphismRestrict p hVr,
90	    Category.assoc, Category.assoc, e]
91	
92	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
93	/-- Precomposition with an `S`-morphism preserves being an `S`-map: if `f` is an
94	`S`-rational map and `p : W ⟶ X` is an `S`-morphism, then `f ∘ p` is an
95	`S`-rational map. In Milne's setting all schemes live over `k̄`, so the difference
96	map is automatically a `k̄`-map. -/
97	instance precomp_isOver {S : Scheme.{u}} [W.Over S] [X.Over S] [Y.Over S]
98	    (f : X.PartialMap Y) [f.IsOver S] (p : W ⟶ X) [p.IsOver S] (hp : IsOpenMap p.base) :
99	    (f.precomp p hp).IsOver S := by
100	  haveI hrestr : (p ∣_ f.domain).IsOver S := by
101	    rw [Hom.isOver_iff]
102	    change (p ∣_ f.domain) ≫ (f.domain.ι ≫ X ↘ S) = (p ⁻¹ᵁ f.domain).ι ≫ W ↘ S
103	    rw [← Category.assoc, morphismRestrict_ι, Category.assoc, comp_over]
104	  change ((p ∣_ f.domain) ≫ f.hom).IsOver S
105	  infer_instance
106	
107	end PartialMap
108	
109	/-- **Precomposition of a rational map with an open morphism.** For `f : X ⤏ Y`
110	and a morphism `p : W ⟶ X` whose underlying map is open, `f.precomp p hp : W ⤏ Y`
111	is the composite `f ∘ p`, obtained by pulling any representative partial map back
112	along `p`. -/
113	noncomputable def RationalMap.precomp (f : X ⤏ Y) (p : W ⟶ X) (hp : IsOpenMap p.base) :
114	    W ⤏ Y :=
115	  Quotient.map (PartialMap.precomp · p hp) (fun _ _ ↦ PartialMap.precomp_equiv p hp) f
116	
117	@[simp]
118	lemma RationalMap.precomp_toRationalMap (f : X.PartialMap Y) (p : W ⟶ X)
119	    (hp : IsOpenMap p.base) :
120	    (f.precomp p hp).toRationalMap = f.toRationalMap.precomp p hp := rfl
121	
122	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
123	/-- Precomposition on the left commutes with right-composition by a morphism.
124	This is what lets Milne's difference map be assembled as
125	`(f.precomp prᵢ).compHom (m ∘ (id × inv))`. -/
126	lemma RationalMap.precomp_compHom (f : X ⤏ Y) (p : W ⟶ X) (hp : IsOpenMap p.base)
127	    (g : Y ⟶ Z) :
128	    (f.precomp p hp).compHom g = (f.compHom g).precomp p hp := by
129	  obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := f.exists_rep
130	  simp only [← RationalMap.precomp_toRationalMap, ← RationalMap.compHom_toRationalMap]
131	  refine congrArg PartialMap.toRationalMap (PartialMap.ext _ _ rfl ?_)
132	  rw [Scheme.isoOfEq_rfl, Iso.refl_hom, Category.id_comp,
133	    PartialMap.compHom_hom, PartialMap.precomp_hom, PartialMap.precomp_hom,
134	    PartialMap.compHom_hom, Category.assoc]
135	  rfl
136	
137	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
138	/-- Precomposing the rational map of a *morphism* `g : X ⟶ Y` with an open morphism
139	`p : W ⟶ X` is the rational map of the composite `p ≫ g`. This produces the over-`S`
140	hypotheses feeding the pairing of Milne's difference map:
141	`(X.hom.toRationalMap).precomp prᵢ = (prᵢ ≫ X.hom).toRationalMap`. -/
142	@[simp]
143	lemma RationalMap.precomp_hom_toRationalMap (g : X ⟶ Y) (p : W ⟶ X)
144	    (hp : IsOpenMap p.base) :
145	    (g.toRationalMap).precomp p hp = (p ≫ g).toRationalMap := by
146	  rw [show (g.toRationalMap : X ⤏ Y) = g.toPartialMap.toRationalMap from rfl,
147	    ← RationalMap.precomp_toRationalMap]
148	  refine congrArg PartialMap.toRationalMap (PartialMap.ext _ _ rfl ?_)
149	  simp only [Scheme.isoOfEq_rfl, Iso.refl_hom, Category.id_comp, PartialMap.precomp_hom,
150	    Scheme.Hom.toPartialMap_hom]
151	  have key : (p ∣_ (g.toPartialMap).domain) ≫ X.topIso.hom = W.topIso.hom ≫ p :=
152	    morphismRestrict_ι p _
153	  rw [← Category.assoc, key, Category.assoc]
154	
155	end Scheme

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Pairing of two rational maps into a fibre product
10	
11	Mathlib's `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap` API provides `compHom`
12	(right-composition with a morphism) but no way to *pair* two rational maps out of
13	a common source into a fibre product. For Milne's difference-map construction
14	`Φ = m ∘ (f × f) : X × X ⤏ G` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Lemma 3.3) one needs
15	exactly this pairing: given `f ∘ pr₁` and `f ∘ pr₂ : X × X ⤏ G`, assemble the map
16	`X × X ⤏ G ×_{k̄} G` whose two projections recover the factors, so that it can be
17	right-composed with the group law `G ×_{k̄} G → G`.
18	
19	The naive `PartialMap`-level pairing via `pullback.lift` does **not** descend to
20	rational maps through `Quotient.map₂`: `RationalMap.IsOver S` guarantees only *one*
21	over-`S` representative, not that an arbitrary pair of representatives is
22	simultaneously over `S`, so the compatibility hypothesis fails. Instead we use the
23	function-field correspondence: over an integral source `X`, a rational map
24	`X ⤏ Y` over `S` is the same datum as an `S`-morphism `Spec K(X) ⟶ Y`
25	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.ofFunctionField` /
26	`equivFunctionField`), and morphisms out of the single scheme `Spec K(X)` pair
27	freely by `pullback.lift`.
28	
29	To stay clear of `Over`-typeclass diamonds on the pullback target we work with
30	explicit structure morphisms `sX : X ⟶ S`, `sY : Y ⟶ S`, `sZ : Z ⟶ S` (matching
31	mathlib's `equivFunctionField`), and phrase the over-`S` datum as the equation
32	`a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap`. In Milne's setting `S = Spec k̄`,
33	`sX = X.hom`, `sY = sZ = G.hom`, and the target `pullback G.hom G.hom` is
34	definitionally `(G ⊗ G).left` (`CategoryTheory.Over.tensorObj_left`), so the group
35	law `(GrpObj.mul).left : (G ⊗ G).left ⟶ G.left` composes on the right directly.
36	
37	## Main definitions
38	
39	* `Scheme.RationalMap.prod` — the pairing `X ⤏ pullback sY sZ` of `a : X ⤏ Y` and
40	  `b : X ⤏ Z`, both over `S`.
41	
42	## Main results
43	
44	* `Scheme.RationalMap.fromFunctionField_compHom` — the function-field morphism of a
45	  right-composition is the composite of function-field morphisms.
46	* `Scheme.RationalMap.prod_compHom_fst` / `prod_compHom_snd` — the two projections of
47	  the pairing recover the original rational maps.
48	* `Scheme.RationalMap.prod_compHom_over` — the pairing is again over `S`.
49	-/
50	
51	set_option autoImplicit false
52	
53	universe u
54	
55	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace
56	
57	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
58	
59	namespace Scheme
60	
61	namespace RationalMap
62	
63	variable {T X Y Z S : Scheme.{u}}
64	
65	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
66	/-- **Associativity of right-composition.** Composing on the right by `g` and then `h`
67	is composing by `g ≫ h`. -/
68	@[simp]
69	lemma compHom_compHom (f : X ⤏ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (h : Z ⟶ T) :
70	    (f.compHom g).compHom h = f.compHom (g ≫ h) := by
71	  obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := f.exists_rep
72	  simp only [← compHom_toRationalMap]
73	  refine congrArg PartialMap.toRationalMap (PartialMap.ext _ _ rfl ?_)
74	  simp only [PartialMap.compHom_hom, Scheme.isoOfEq_rfl, Iso.refl_hom, Category.id_comp,
75	    Category.assoc]
76	
77	/-- **Function-field functoriality of right-composition.** For a rational map
78	`f : X ⤏ Y` out of an irreducible scheme and a morphism `g : Y ⟶ Z`, the associated
79	morphism `Spec K(X) ⟶ Z` of `f.compHom g` is `f.fromFunctionField ≫ g`. -/
80	@[simp]
81	lemma fromFunctionField_compHom [IrreducibleSpace X] (f : X ⤏ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) :
82	    (f.compHom g).fromFunctionField = f.fromFunctionField ≫ g := by
83	  obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := f.exists_rep
84	  rw [← compHom_toRationalMap, fromFunctionField_toRationalMap, fromFunctionField_toRationalMap]
85	  exact PartialMap.fromSpecStalkOfMem_compHom f g _ _
86	
87	/-- The function-field morphism of an over-`S` rational map factors the structure
88	morphism: if `a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap`, then
89	`a.fromFunctionField ≫ sY = X.fromSpecStalk η ≫ sX`. -/
90	lemma fromFunctionField_comp_structure [IsIntegral X] (a : X ⤏ Y) (sX : X ⟶ S)
91	    (sY : Y ⟶ S) (ha : a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap) :
92	    a.fromFunctionField ≫ sY = X.fromSpecStalk (genericPoint X) ≫ sX := by
93	  rw [← fromFunctionField_compHom, ha]
94	  exact PartialMap.fromSpecStalkOfMem_toPartialMap sX _
95	
96	/-- **Pairing of two rational maps into a fibre product.** For rational maps
97	`a : X ⤏ Y` and `b : X ⤏ Z` out of an integral scheme `X`, both defined over `S`
98	(encoded as `a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap`, resp. for `b`), the pairing
99	`a.prod b : X ⤏ pullback sY sZ` is the rational map whose function-field morphism is
100	`pullback.lift a.fromFunctionField b.fromFunctionField`.
101	
102	Milne's difference map is `(f.precomp pr₁).prod (f.precomp pr₂)` followed by
103	right-composition with the group law of `G`. -/
104	noncomputable def prod (sX : X ⟶ S) (sY : Y ⟶ S) (sZ : Z ⟶ S)
105	    [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY] [LocallyOfFiniteType sZ]
106	    (a : X ⤏ Y) (b : X ⤏ Z)
107	    (ha : a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap) (hb : b.compHom sZ = sX.toRationalMap) :
108	    X ⤏ pullback sY sZ :=
109	  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType (pullback.fst sY sZ ≫ sY) :=
110	    MorphismProperty.comp_mem _ _ _ inferInstance inferInstance
111	  ofFunctionField sX (pullback.fst sY sZ ≫ sY)
112	    (pullback.lift a.fromFunctionField b.fromFunctionField
113	      ((fromFunctionField_comp_structure a sX sY ha).trans
114	        (fromFunctionField_comp_structure b sX sZ hb).symm))
115	    (by rw [pullback.lift_fst_assoc]; exact fromFunctionField_comp_structure a sX sY ha)
116	
117	/-- The function-field morphism of the pairing is the `pullback.lift` of the factors'
118	function-field morphisms. -/
119	@[simp]
120	lemma prod_fromFunctionField (sX : X ⟶ S) (sY : Y ⟶ S) (sZ : Z ⟶ S)
121	    [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY] [LocallyOfFiniteType sZ]
122	    (a : X ⤏ Y) (b : X ⤏ Z)
123	    (ha : a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap) (hb : b.compHom sZ = sX.toRationalMap) :
124	    (prod sX sY sZ a b ha hb).fromFunctionField
125	      = pullback.lift a.fromFunctionField b.fromFunctionField
126	        ((fromFunctionField_comp_structure a sX sY ha).trans
127	          (fromFunctionField_comp_structure b sX sZ hb).symm) := by
128	  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType (pullback.fst sY sZ ≫ sY) :=
129	    MorphismProperty.comp_mem _ _ _ inferInstance inferInstance
130	  exact fromFunctionField_ofFunctionField _ _ _ _
131	
132	/-- **First projection of the pairing.** Right-composing the pairing with
133	`pullback.fst` recovers `a`. -/
134	lemma prod_compHom_fst (sX : X ⟶ S) (sY : Y ⟶ S) (sZ : Z ⟶ S)
135	    [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY] [LocallyOfFiniteType sZ]
136	    (a : X ⤏ Y) (b : X ⤏ Z)
137	    (ha : a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap) (hb : b.compHom sZ = sX.toRationalMap) :
138	    (prod sX sY sZ a b ha hb).compHom (pullback.fst sY sZ) = a :=
139	  eq_of_fromFunctionField_eq _ _ (by
140	    rw [fromFunctionField_compHom, prod_fromFunctionField, pullback.lift_fst])
141	
142	/-- **Second projection of the pairing.** Right-composing the pairing with
143	`pullback.snd` recovers `b`. -/
144	lemma prod_compHom_snd (sX : X ⟶ S) (sY : Y ⟶ S) (sZ : Z ⟶ S)
145	    [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY] [LocallyOfFiniteType sZ]
146	    (a : X ⤏ Y) (b : X ⤏ Z)
147	    (ha : a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap) (hb : b.compHom sZ = sX.toRationalMap) :
148	    (prod sX sY sZ a b ha hb).compHom (pullback.snd sY sZ) = b :=
149	  eq_of_fromFunctionField_eq _ _ (by
150	    rw [fromFunctionField_compHom, prod_fromFunctionField, pullback.lift_snd])
151	
152	/-- **The pairing is over `S`.** Right-composing with the structure morphism
153	`pullback.fst sY sZ ≫ sY` of the fibre product recovers `sX.toRationalMap`. -/
154	lemma prod_compHom_over (sX : X ⟶ S) (sY : Y ⟶ S) (sZ : Z ⟶ S)
155	    [IsIntegral X] [LocallyOfFiniteType sY] [LocallyOfFiniteType sZ]
156	    (a : X ⤏ Y) (b : X ⤏ Z)
157	    (ha : a.compHom sY = sX.toRationalMap) (hb : b.compHom sZ = sX.toRationalMap) :
158	    (prod sX sY sZ a b ha hb).compHom (pullback.fst sY sZ ≫ sY) = sX.toRationalMap := by
159	  rw [← compHom_compHom, prod_compHom_fst, ha]
160	
161	end RationalMap
162	
163	end Scheme
164	
165	end AlgebraicGeometry

abbrev Hom.toRationalMap (f : X.Hom Y) : X ⤏ Y := f.toPartialMap.toRationalMap

variable (S) in
/-- A rational map is an `S`-map if some partial map in the equivalence class is an `S`-map. -/
class RationalMap.IsOver [X.Over S] [Y.Over S] (f : X ⤏ Y) : Prop where
  exists_partialMap_over : ∃ g : X.PartialMap Y, g.IsOver S ∧ g.toRationalMap = f

lemma PartialMap.toRationalMap_surjective : Function.Surjective (@toRationalMap X Y) :=
  Quotient.exists_rep

lemma RationalMap.exists_rep (f : X ⤏ Y) : ∃ g : X.PartialMap Y, g.toRationalMap = f :=
  Quotient.exists_rep f

lemma PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff {f g : X.PartialMap Y} :
    f.toRationalMap = g.toRationalMap ↔ f.equiv g :=
  Quotient.eq

/-- An arbitrarily chosen partial map representing `f`. Use `RationalMap.toPartialMap` instead
if `X` is reduced and `Y` is separated. -/
noncomputable def RationalMap.representative (f : X ⤏ Y) : X.PartialMap Y :=
  f.exists_rep.choose

@[simp]
lemma RationalMap.toRationalMap_representative (f : X ⤏ Y) :
    f.representative.toRationalMap = f :=
  f.exists_rep.choose_spec

lemma PartialMap.representative_toRationalMap_equiv (f : X.PartialMap Y) :
    f.toRationalMap.representative.equiv f := by
  rw [← PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff, f.toRationalMap.toRationalMap_representative]

@[simp]
lemma PartialMap.restrict_toRationalMap (f : X.PartialMap Y) (U : X.Opens)
    (hU : Dense (U : Set X)) (hU' : U ≤ f.domain) :
    (f.restrict U hU hU').toRationalMap = f.toRationalMap :=
  toRationalMap_eq_iff.mpr (f.restrict_equiv U hU hU')

instance [X.Over S] [Y.Over S] (f : X.PartialMap Y) [f.IsOver S] : f.toRationalMap.IsOver S :=
  ⟨f, ‹_›, rfl⟩

variable (S) in
lemma RationalMap.exists_partialMap_over [X.Over S] [Y.Over S] (f : X ⤏ Y) [f.IsOver S] :
    ∃ g : X.PartialMap Y, g.IsOver S ∧ g.toRationalMap = f :=
  IsOver.exists_partialMap_over

set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
/-- The composition of a rational map and a morphism on the right. -/
def RationalMap.compHom (f : X ⤏ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) : X ⤏ Z := by
  refine Quotient.map (PartialMap.compHom · g) ?_ f
  intro f₁ f₂ ⟨W, hW, hWl, hWr, e⟩
  refine ⟨W, hW, hWl, hWr, ?_⟩
  simp only [PartialMap.restrict_domain, PartialMap.restrict_hom, PartialMap.compHom_domain,
    PartialMap.compHom_hom] at e ⊢
  rw [reassoc_of% e]

@[simp]
lemma RationalMap.compHom_toRationalMap (f : X.PartialMap Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) :
    (f.compHom g).toRationalMap = f.toRationalMap.compHom g := rfl

instance [X.Over S] [Y.Over S] [Z.Over S] (f : X ⤏ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z)
    [f.IsOver S] [g.IsOver S] : (f.compHom g).IsOver S where
  exists_partialMap_over := by
    obtain ⟨f, hf, rfl⟩ := f.exists_partialMap_over S
    exact ⟨f.compHom g, inferInstance, rfl⟩

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
variable (S) in
lemma PartialMap.exists_restrict_isOver [X.Over S] [Y.Over S] (f : X.PartialMap Y)
    [f.toRationalMap.IsOver S] : ∃ U hU hU', (f.restrict U hU hU').IsOver S := by
  obtain ⟨f', hf₁, hf₂⟩ := RationalMap.IsOver.exists_partialMap_over (S := S) (f := f.toRationalMap)
  obtain ⟨U, hU, hUl, hUr, e⟩ := PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp hf₂
  exact ⟨U, hU, hUr, by rw [IsOver, ← e]; infer_instance⟩

set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
lemma RationalMap.isOver_iff [X.Over S] [Y.Over S] {f : X ⤏ Y} :
    f.IsOver S ↔ f.compHom (Y ↘ S) = (X ↘ S).toRationalMap := by

    obtain ⟨g, hg, e⟩ := f.exists_partialMap_over S

  · intro e
    obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := PartialMap.toRationalMap_surjective f
    obtain ⟨U, hU, hUl, hUr, e⟩ := PartialMap.toRationalMap_eq_iff.mp e
    exact ⟨⟨f.restrict U hU hUl, by simpa using! e, by simp⟩⟩

set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
lemma PartialMap.isOver_toRationalMap_iff_of_isSeparated [X.Over S] [Y.Over S] [IsReduced X]
    [S.IsSeparated] {f : X.PartialMap Y} :
    f.toRationalMap.IsOver S ↔ f.IsOver S := by
  refine ⟨fun _ ↦ ?_, fun _ ↦ inferInstance⟩
  obtain ⟨U, hU, hU', H⟩ := f.exists_restrict_isOver (S := S)
  rw [isOver_iff]
  have : IsDominant (X.homOfLE hU') := Opens.isDominant_homOfLE hU _
  exact ext_of_isDominant (ι := X.homOfLE hU') (by simpa using H.1)

namespace Scheme.RationalMap

/-- **Right-composition with a morphism only enlarges the domain of definition.**
For a rational map `F : X ⤏ Y` and a morphism `g : Y ⟶ Z`, every point at which
`F` is defined is also a point at which `F.compHom g` is defined: a partial-map
representative `F₀` of `F` yields the representative `F₀.compHom g` of
`F.compHom g` with the *same* domain (post-composing with a total morphism never
destroys definedness). This is Milne Lemma 3.3, Sub-step 1: the difference map
`Φ = (prod ⋯).compHom (diff_G)` is defined wherever the pairing `prod ⋯` is. -/
theorem le_domain_compHom {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (F : X.RationalMap Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) :
    F.domain ≤ (F.compHom g).domain := by
  intro x hx

/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3, Sub-step 1 (domain lower bound / Sub-step 2 easy direction).**
The difference rational map `Φ` is defined on `Dom(f) ×_{k̄} Dom(f)`: every point
`p` of `X ×_{k̄} X` projecting into `Dom(f)` under both projections lies in
`Dom(Φ)`. This is Milne's "clearly `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)` if `f` is defined at
`x`" — indeed at every `(x, y)` with `x, y ∈ Dom(f)` — made precise: a maximal
representative `(U, φ_U)` of `f` (`f.toPartialMap`) supplies the honest morphism
`U ×_{k̄} U → G` of Milne's construction, which represents `Φ`. -/
theorem le_domain_differenceRationalMap
    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left)
    (hover : f.compHom G.hom = X.hom.toRationalMap)
    [IsIntegral (pullback X.hom X.hom)] [IsReduced X.left] [G.left.IsSeparated] :
    (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ f.domain) ⊓ (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ f.domain)
      ≤ (differenceRationalMap f hover).domain := by
  -- Local `Scheme.Over` structure on the underlying schemes, to invoke the
  -- over-representative machinery of Mathlib's rational-map API.
  letI : X.left.Over (Spec (.of kbar)) := ⟨X.hom⟩
  letI : G.left.Over (Spec (.of kbar)) := ⟨G.hom⟩
  haveI hfover : f.IsOver (Spec (.of kbar)) := RationalMap.isOver_iff.mpr hover
  -- Over-ness of the maximal representative `f₀ = f.toPartialMap`.
  have hover₀ : f.toPartialMap.hom ≫ G.hom
      = f.toPartialMap.domain.ι ≫ X.hom := by
    have h := (PartialMap.isOver_iff (S := Spec (.of kbar))
      (f := f.toPartialMap)).mp inferInstance
    simpa [PartialMap.compHom] using h
  -- Reduce to the pairing `prod`, via right-composition monotonicity.
  refine le_trans ?_ (le_domain_compHom _ (grpObjDiffLeft G))
  sorry

end Scheme.RationalMap

    f.dense_domain.preimage (isOpenMap_pullback_fst_self X)

    f.dense_domain.preimage (isOpenMap_pullback_snd_self X)
  -- The two precomposed partial-map legs from the maximal representative `f₀`.
  set a₀ := f.toPartialMap.precomp (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom)
    (isOpenMap_pullback_fst_self X) with ha₀
  set b₀ := f.toPartialMap.precomp (pullback.snd X.hom X.hom)

  have ha₀dom : a₀.domain = pullback.fst X.hom X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ f.domain := by
    rw [ha₀, PartialMap.precomp_domain, toPartialMap_domain]
  have hb₀dom : b₀.domain = pullback.snd X.hom X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ f.domain := by
    rw [hb₀, PartialMap.precomp_domain, toPartialMap_domain]
  have hDdense : Dense ((a₀.domain ⊓ b₀.domain : (pullback X.hom X.hom).Opens) : Set _) := by
    rw [ha₀dom, hb₀dom]
    exact hd₁.inter_of_isOpen_left hd₂ (pullback.fst X.hom X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ f.domain).2
